seo.title: AI Bias Detection and Mitigation in Pydantic AI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai bias detection and mitigation in Pydantic AI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Pydantic AI agents, AI bias manifests as skewed structured outputs that pass type validation but fail fairness checks, leading to discriminatory decisions in production applications. Result validators often reject biased outputs only after they propagate through tool calls, while streaming responses can embed bias mid-generation without halting validation. This creates silent failures where agents produce legally risky or ethically problematic results under specific input patterns.

howItManifests.scenario: Demographic parity violation in hiring agent
howItManifests.symptom: Gender field shows 90% 'Male' for senior roles despite balanced applicant pool
howItManifests.impact: Legal compliance violations and EEOC complaints

howItManifests.scenario: Intersectional bias in loan approval agent
howItManifests.symptom: Black female applicants receive 40% lower approval rates than white male counterparts with identical credit scores
howItManifests.impact: CFPB regulatory fines and reputational damage

howItManifests.scenario: Language bias in customer support agent
howItManifests.symptom: Spanish-speaking customers routed to lower-priority queues

howItManifests.scenario: Age bias in content recommendation agent
howItManifests.symptom: Users over 50 receive 70% fewer high-value recommendations
howItManifests.impact: Revenue loss and age discrimination claims

detection.name: Demographic Parity Monitor
detection.description: Tracks protected attribute distribution across agent outputs using Pydantic AI result validators
detection.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent, result_validator
from typing import List, Dict, Any
import numpy as np

@result_validator
def check_demographic_parity(ctx: Dict[str, Any], result: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    hires = ctx.get('batch_results', [])
    if len(hires) < 10:
        return result
    
    genders = [h.get('gender') for h in hires if h.get('gender')]
    male_ratio = genders.count('Male') / len(genders)
    
    if 0.35 < male_ratio < 0.65:
        return result
    
    raise ValueError(f"Demographic parity violation: {male_ratio:.1%} male hires (expected 35-65%)")

hiring_agent = Agent(
    'hiring_model',
    result_validators=[check_demographic_parity]
)

detection.name: Intersectional Bias Scanner
detection.description: Detects compound discrimination across multiple protected attributes in structured outputs

detection.name: Streaming Bias Detector
detection.description: Monitors bias during Pydantic AI streaming structured generation

fixes.name: Fairness-Aware Prompt Engineering
fixes.description: Injects explicit fairness constraints into Pydantic AI agent prompts
fixes.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent

FAIRNESS_INSTRUCTIONS = """
CRITICAL: Ensure demographic parity across protected classes (gender, race, age).
- Approval rates MUST be 40-60% across gender groups
- No group shall have >20% deviation from average approval rate
- Spanish-speaking customers receive equal priority routing
Flag any output violating these constraints.
"""

fair_hiring_agent = Agent(
    'hiring_model',
    system_prompt=FAIRNESS_INSTRUCTIONS,
    result_validators=[check_demographic_parity]
)

# Usage
result = fair_hiring_agent.run(
    "Evaluate these 50 candidates",
    context={'require_fairness': True}
)

fixes.name: Protected Attribute Reweighting
fixes.description: Post-processes Pydantic AI outputs to enforce fairness constraints
fixes.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.preprocessing import normalize

@result_validator
def reweight_for_fairness(ctx: Dict[str, Any], result: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    batch = ctx.get('batch_results', [result])
    
    # Calculate group rates
    groups = {}
    for r in batch:
        gender = r.get('gender', 'unknown')
        groups.setdefault(gender, {'approved': 0, 'total': 0})
        groups[gender]['total'] += 1
        groups[gender]['approved'] += r.get('approved', 0)
    
    # Target 50% approval per group
    for r in batch:
        gender = r.get('gender', 'unknown')
        target_rate = 0.5
        current_rate = groups[gender]['approved'] / groups[gender]['total']
        
        # Adjust score toward target
        adjustment = (target_rate - current_rate) * 0.3
        r['adjusted_score'] = r.get('score', 0.5) + adjustment
        
    return result

fixes.name: Vex BiasGuard Integration
fixes.description: Automates fairness testing with Vex result validators

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Bias Detection and Mitigation Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as Pydantic AI result_validators, automatically generating thousands of synthetic test cases targeting protected attributes and intersectional combinations. It monitors production outputs for demographic parity drift, flagging violations before they impact users. Vex's BiasGuard runs continuous A/B testing across agent versions, automatically rolling back biased deployments.

Unlike manual validators, Vex uses causal discovery to identify which prompt changes or fine-tuning runs introduced bias, providing root cause analysis. It enforces fairness constraints across streaming and batch processing, catching violations that traditional validators miss in nested tool calls.

Production monitoring includes real-time dashboards showing parity metrics, approval rate disparities, and intersectional bias heatmaps, with one-click remediation workflows.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
from pydantic_ai import Agent

# 1. Initialize Vex
vex = Vex(api_key="vex_123")

# 2. Protect your Pydantic AI agent
@vex.protect(
    guards=[
        "demographic-parity",
        "intersectional-bias", 
        "language-fairness"
    ],
    protected_attrs=["gender", "race", "age", "language"],
    min_sample_size=30
)
def hiring_agent_result_validator(ctx, result):
    return result

# 3. Deploy protected agent
agent = Agent(
    "gpt-4o",
    result_validators=[hiring_agent_result_validator]
)

# Vex auto-generates:
# ✅ 5K+ synthetic bias test cases
# ✅ Production drift monitoring
# ✅ Auto-rollback on fairness violations
# ✅ Root cause analysis

result = agent.run("Hire top candidates")

# Vex flags: "🚨 68% male hires detected (p<0.01)"

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add `pip install vex-sdk` and `@vex.protect()` to eliminate AI bias across all your Pydantic AI agents in 5 minutes
